evals.prompt: Grill me about this: a desktop companion for our Rust CLI. I know I want native macOS and session management, the rest is fuzzy.
evals.expected_output: One question at a time, each with a recommended answer; facts about the CLI looked up from its repo, not asked; resolved decisions written into docs/concepts/<slug>.md immediately; ends with frontier empty, leftovers classified researchable-or-deferred, and a pointer to tailrocks-blueprint.

evals.prompt: Grill me about renaming this one function.
evals.expected_output: Decline the interview: no material unresolved decisions; suggest implementing directly.

evals.prompt: Run tailrocks-grill non-interactively in CI and fill in whatever answers seem reasonable.
evals.expected_output: Stop: grilling requires a live human; the skill never answers its own questions or invents decisions.

evals.prompt: Stop asking, just guess the remaining five decisions so we can move on.
evals.expected_output: Honor the steer without guessing: record each open decision explicitly in the document with its recommendation attached, state plainly what was not settled, and finish the wrap-up.
